mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in CPIAUCSL signals easing inflation pressure, which keeps real discount rates from rising and supports equity multiples. When inflation is falling, S&P 500 returns are positive over the next 20 trading days because rate-sensitive valuation compression is reduced.
failure_condition: Reject if CPIAUCSL declines are followed by negative 20d SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in FEDFUNDS above a restrictive threshold tightens financial conditions and lifts the equity discount rate, so S&P 500 forward returns turn negative over the next 20 trading days. The signal is strongest after the policy rate crosses into clearly restrictive territory.
failure_condition: Reject if SPX 20d returns are positive in more than 50% of observations after FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or if the thresholded signal has no incremental predictive value versus unconditional returns.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in UNRATE combined with weakening PAYEMS marks a labor-market deterioration that reduces expected earnings growth and raises recession risk. When unemployment rises and payroll growth stalls together, S&P 500 returns are negative over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising UNRATE and flat or falling PAYEMS does not produce lower 20d SPX returns than each series separately, or if the interaction term is not significant in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS accelerates after a prior lull, it signals improving nominal demand and better near-term revenue growth for listed firms. That demand reacceleration should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth turns positive after 2 consecutive monthly declines
failure_condition: Reject if RSAFS reacceleration is not followed by positive 5d SPX returns more often than not, or if the signal’s 1-year rolling hit rate falls below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO indicates stronger industrial output and firmer real activity, which supports cyclical earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The effect should be observable as a delayed equity response to sustained production strength.
failure_condition: Reject if above-trend INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 60d SPX returns over any 3-year rolling window, or if the out-of-sample slope is indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in HOUST below a low-building threshold signals a housing downturn, which weakens construction, consumer confidence, and credit-sensitive demand. Under that condition, S&P 500 returns are negative over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if low-HOUST episodes are not associated with lower 60d SPX returns than baseline, or if the threshold signal loses significance after accounting for the prior 6-month trend.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS weakens while UNRATE rises, labor income growth stalls and household spending momentum fades, which leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The interaction is required because payroll weakness alone is less informative than payroll weakness paired with rising unemployment.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined PAYEMS decline and UNRATE increase does not outperform either standalone series in predicting negative 20d SPX returns, or if the interaction coefficient flips sign in rolling estimation.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rebound in GDPC1 and RSAFS identifies a broad demand upswing that should feed through to better earnings revisions and higher S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The signal is strongest when both real output and sales are improving together.
failure_condition: Reject if joint GDPC1 and RSAFS improvement does not produce positive 60d SPX returns more often than single-series signals, or if the interaction fails to improve out-of-sample Rank IC above 0.03.
